title: scaevola cumana
intro: Scaevola cumana, also known as the sea lavender or beach morning glory, is a flowering plant native to tropical Australia and Southeast Asia. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral effects. The health benefits of Scaevola cumana include its ability to soothe skin irritations, reduce anxiety and stress, and aid in the healing of wounds. In herbal preparations, the plant can be made into teas, infusions, and topical creams, allowing for its medicinal properties to be harnessed for a range of health benefits.
